name: RDP Brute-Force Attack
severity: High (especially for privileged or sensitive systems)
detection: SIEM, Windows Security Event Logs, EDR, IDS/IPS, Firewall logs,
scenario: An attacker launches a brute-force or password spraying attack against internet-exposed or internal RDP services to gain access using weak or reused credentials. Successful access may lead to lateral movement, malware deployment or data exfiltration.
tools: SIEM (e.g., Splunk, Sentinel, QRadar); EDR (e.g., CrowdStrike, Microsoft Defender for Endpoint, Cortex XDR); Firewall and VPN logs (e.g., Fortinet, Palo Alto, Cisco ASA); Windows Event Viewer (Security Logs: 4624, 4625, 4648, 4672); Threat intelligence platforms (for IP enrichment); Brute-force dete

detSteps.title: Confirm brute-force pattern and volume of failed attempts
detSteps.detail: RDP brute force produces high volumes of failed authentication against the same destination IP on port 3389. Look for Event ID 4625 (failed login) with many distinct usernames or the same username repeated. Password spraying shows one attempt per account across many accounts.

queries.splunk: index=network sourcetype=wineventlog EventCode=4625 dest_port=3389 | stats count dc(user) as users by src_ip | where count > 50 | sort -count
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where EventID == 4625
| where LogonType == 10
| summarize FailCount=count(), UniqueUsers=dcount(TargetUserName) by IpAddress
| where FailCount > 50
| order by FailCount desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, COUNT(*) as failures, COUNT(DISTINCT username) as users FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%' AND qid IN (SELECT qid FROM qidmap WHERE eventid = '4625') GROUP BY sourceip HAVING failures > 50 ORDER BY failures DESC LAST 1 HOURS
queries.velociraptor: SELECT EventID, TimeCreated, Computer, Channel, Message FROM Artifact.Windows.EventLogs.SecurityEventLogParser() ORDER BY TimeCreated DESC LIMIT 200

detSteps.title: Check for successful logins following the failure spike — brute force success
detSteps.detail: A successful login (Event ID 4624) from the same attacker IP shortly after a failure spike indicates the brute force succeeded. This is the critical escalation point — treat the destination host as fully compromised.

queries.splunk: index=network sourcetype=wineventlog EventCode IN (4624,4625) src_ip=[attacker_ip] | stats count(eval(EventCode=4624)) as success count(eval(EventCode=4625)) as fail by user | where success > 0
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where IpAddress == '<attacker_ip>'
| where EventID in (4624, 4625)
| summarize Success=countif(EventID==4624), Failures=countif(EventID==4625) by TargetUserName, IpAddress
| where Success > 0
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, username, SUM(CASE WHEN qid IN (SELECT qid FROM qidmap WHERE eventid='4624') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as success, SUM(CASE WHEN qid IN (SELECT qid FROM qidmap WHERE eventid='4625') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as failures FROM events WHERE sourceip = '<attacker_ip>' AND logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%' GROUP BY sourceip, username LAST 1 HOURS
queries.velociraptor: SELECT EventID, TimeCreated, Computer, Channel, Message FROM Artifact.Windows.EventLogs.SecurityEventLogParser() ORDER BY TimeCreated DESC LIMIT 200

detSteps.title: Analyse attacker IP reputation and geographic origin
detSteps.detail: Check the attacker's IP geolocation and ASN. Most RDP brute force comes from known VPS providers (DigitalOcean, Linode, OVH), compromised residential IPs, or TOR exit nodes. Geolocation helps build blocking rules and assess whether other assets are targeted.

queries.splunk: index=network sourcetype=wineventlog EventCode=4625 dest_port=3389 | stats count by src_ip | sort -count | head 20
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where EventID == 4625
| where LogonType == 10
| summarize Failures=count() by IpAddress
| order by Failures desc
| take 20
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, COUNT(*) as failures FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%' AND qid IN (SELECT qid FROM qidmap WHERE eventid = '4625') GROUP BY sourceip ORDER BY failures DESC LAST 1 HOURS
queries.velociraptor: SELECT EventID, TimeCreated, Computer, Channel, Message FROM Artifact.Windows.EventLogs.SecurityEventLogParser() ORDER BY TimeCreated DESC LIMIT 200

contSteps.title: Block attacker IPs at the firewall and VPN gateway
contSteps.detail: Apply deny rules at the perimeter for the attacker's source IPs. If RDP is exposed directly to the internet, immediately restrict access to approved IP ranges or require VPN. Temporarily disable RDP on high-risk systems that were targeted until secured.

queries.kql:
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where RemotePort == 3389
| where RemoteIP in (<attacker_ips>)
| summarize Connections=count() by RemoteIP, DeviceName
| order by Connections desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, destinationip, COUNT(*) as conn FROM events WHERE destinationport = 3389 AND sourceip IN (<attacker_ips>) AND logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Firewall%' GROUP BY sourceip, destinationip ORDER BY conn DESC LAST 1 HOURS

contSteps.title: Lock or reset accounts targeted by the brute force
contSteps.detail: Force a password reset on all accounts targeted — especially if any succeeded. Enforce account lockout policy (maximum 5 failed attempts, 30-minute lockout). Notify users if their account was targeted.

queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where EventID in (4624, 4625)
| where LogonType == 10
| project TimeGenerated, TargetUserName, IpAddress, EventID, WorkstationName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, sourceip, QIDNAME(qid) as event, starttime FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%' AND qid IN (SELECT qid FROM qidmap WHERE eventid IN ('4624','4625')) ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS

eradSteps.title: Remove unauthorised access: Kill sessions, reset passwords and revoke tokens or certificates

queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where EventID == 4624
| where LogonType == 10
| where IpAddress in (<attacker_ips>)
| project TimeGenerated, TargetUserName, IpAddress, WorkstationName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, sourceip, starttime FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%' AND qid IN (SELECT qid FROM qidmap WHERE eventid = '4624') AND sourceip IN (<attacker_ips>) ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS

eradSteps.title: Patch exposed systems: Update RDP services and OS to prevent exploits (e.g.,

queries.kql:
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where RemotePort == 3389
| summarize Connections=count() by DeviceName, RemoteIP
| order by Connections desc
queries.qradar: SELECT destinationip, sourceip, COUNT(*) as conn FROM events WHERE destinationport = 3389 AND logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Firewall%' GROUP BY destinationip, sourceip ORDER BY conn DESC LAST 24 HOURS

queries.kql:
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where RegistryKey has_any ('Run','RunOnce','Policies\\Explorer\\Run')
| project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, "Registry Key" as regkey, QIDNAME(qid) as event, starttime FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Sysmon%' AND QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%Registry%' ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS

eradSteps.title: Validate no lateral movement: Check for new scheduled tasks, services or registry keys added by attacker

queries.kql:
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where RemotePort in (445, 3389, 139)
| summarize Targets=dcount(RemoteIP) by DeviceName
| where Targets > 5
| order by Targets desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, destinationip, destinationport, COUNT(*) as conn FROM events WHERE destinationport IN (445, 3389, 139) AND logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Firewall%' GROUP BY sourceip, destinationip, destinationport ORDER BY conn DESC LAST 24 HOURS

eradSteps.title: Use EDR or log review to ensure attacker did not spread internally

queries.kql:
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(48h)
| where RemotePort in (445, 3389, 139)
| where DeviceName =~ '<affected_host>'
| summarize Targets=dcount(RemoteIP) by RemotePort, InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by Targets desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, destinationip, destinationport, COUNT(*) as conn FROM events WHERE sourceip = '<affected_ip>' AND destinationport IN (445, 3389, 139) GROUP BY sourceip, destinationip, destinationport ORDER BY conn DESC LAST 48 HOURS

recSteps.title: Reinstate secure RDP access: Only via VPN or bastion host with MFA
Notify users or IT teams: Alert those impacted by the attempted logins or credential resets
Monitor closely postincident: Watch for continued brute-force activity or targeted retries

queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID in (4624, 4625)
| where LogonType == 10
| summarize count() by IpAddress, TargetUserName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT sourceip, username, COUNT(*) as attempts FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%' AND qid IN (SELECT qid FROM qidmap WHERE eventid IN ('4624','4625')) GROUP BY sourceip, username ORDER BY attempts DESC LAST 7 DAYS

recSteps.title: Conduct password audit: Prompt company-wide password hygiene checks if weak credentials were used

metrics.name: Credential Reset Time
metrics.target: <2 hours for compromised or targeted accounts

metrics.name: Exposure Time
metrics.target: No unauthorised RDP access exceeding 15 minutes

metrics.name: RDP Lockdown Coverage
metrics.target: 100% of internet-facing RDP endpoints secured or removed
